findings.evidence: mapsTo_fiber uses Set.mem_singleton_iff which is the only singleton-specific rewrite; removing it and {b}→s gives valid Set.MapsTo φ.1 (p⁻¹' s) (p⁻¹' s). fiberHomeomorph uses eq_comm solely because =b goals get normalized differently; the s version avoids this entirely (goal is p e ∈ s ↔ p e ∈ s after map_proj).
findings.fix: Generalize all three to take (s : Set B) instead of (b : B). The proofs become: mapsTo_preimage: simpa [Set.mem_preimage, map_proj] using he; preimageHomeomorph: φ.1.subtype fun e => by simp [Set.mem_preimage, map_proj]. Derive the singleton versions from them if still needed.
findings.issue: mapsTo_fiber (L57), mapsTo_fiber_symm (L63), and fiberHomeomorph (L70) are all specialized to p⁻¹'{b} when their proofs work for p⁻¹'s for any s : Set B. The eq_comm in fiberHomeomorph is a tell-tale sign of the unnecessary specialization; the general proof is simpler.

summary: Three declarations in Deck.lean (mapsTo_fiber, mapsTo_fiber_symm, fiberHomeomorph) are proved only for singleton preimages p⁻¹'{b} when their proofs work identically for p⁻¹'s for any s : Set B. The singleton versions are unnecessary special cases; the general set versions should be the primary API, with the singleton versions derived if needed.
